title: phyllanthus oppositifolius
intro: Phyllanthus oppositifolius, also known as Ma'afala, is a tropical plant species native to the Pacific Islands, characterized by its large, oval-shaped leaves that grow opposite each other on the stem. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Phyllanthus oppositifolius include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting liver health. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made from the leaves, roots, or fruits of the plant.
